Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me compile the report.

## Summary Report: Video Submission Functionality

### File Summaries

#### 1. **video-submission-form.tsx** (Client Component)
**What it does:**
- Main form UI for athletes to submit video + score for competition events
- Handles form state management (video URL, score, notes, secondary/tiebreak scores)
- Manages submission/preview mode transitions with success/error handling
- Displays submission window status and athlete registration checks

**Key exports:**
- `VideoSubmissionForm` component (main export)

**Business logic to test:**
- Score parsing/validation via `parseScore()` for different schemes (time, rounds-reps, reps, load, etc.)
- Time cap detection: scores >= cap trigger secondary score field and status="cap"
- Tiebreak value parsing (converts to milliseconds for time scheme)
- Form validation: URL must be valid, score must parse correctly, notes max 1000 chars
- Submission window status display (not registered, not yet open, closed, etc.)
- Edit/preview mode transitions with success message delay
- Score status derivation (scored vs cap)

**Edge cases:**
- Over-cap time submissions (5:30 when cap is 5:00)
- Whitespace-only inputs are trimmed before submit
- Notes can be null or empty string (converted to undefined before send)
- No score submission allowed if score validation fails
- Secondary score only saved when capped; tiebreak only when scheme has tiebreak

---

#### 2. **video-submission-preview.tsx** (Client Component)
**What it does:**
- Read-only display of submitted video + score details
- Shows YouTube embedded player for YouTube URLs, link for others
- Displays score with secondary/tiebreak values when present
- Shows submission timestamps and edit status
- Provides edit button if submission window still open

**Key exports:**
- `VideoSubmissionPreview` component

**Business logic to test:**
- YouTube URL detection via `isYouTubeUrl()`
- Tiebreak time formatting (milliseconds ‚Üí M:SS format)
- Submission vs updated timestamps (shows "Updated" only if different)
- Edit button enabled only when `canEdit=true` and `onEdit` provided
- Safe URL rendering via `isSafeUrl()` for all links
- Score display includes "Capped" badge when status="cap"

**Edge cases:**
- Non-YouTube video URLs fallback to external link with icon
- Notes displayed with whitespace preserved via `whitespace-pre-wrap`
- Secondary value shown only when status="cap" AND secondaryValue !== null
- Tiebreak shown only when both tiebreakValue !== null AND tiebreakScheme exists

---

#### 3. **youtube-embed.tsx** (Client Component)
**What it does:**
- Extracts YouTube video ID from various URL formats
- Renders responsive iframe with sandbox restrictions
- Provides fallback to external link if URL can't be parsed
- Includes YouTube thumbnail component with error fallback

**Key exports:**
- `YouTubeEmbed` component
- `isYouTubeUrl()` function (checks if URL is YouTube)
- `YouTubeThumbnail` component

**Business logic to test:**
- `extractYouTubeVideoId()`: supports watch?v=, youtu.be/, /embed/, /shorts/ formats
- URL.constructor() parsing with fallback to regex if URL parsing fails
- Video ID validation: must be exactly 11 alphanumeric chars with dash/underscore
- Regex patterns: `/(?:youtube\.com\/watch\?v=|youtu\.be\/|youtube\.com\/embed\/|youtube\.com\/shorts\/)([a-zA-Z0-9_-]{11})/`
- Thumbnail loading with maxresdefault.jpg fallback to mqdefault.jpg on error
- iframe sandbox: allows scripts, same-origin, presentation
- URL parsing errors caught silently with graceful fallback

**Edge cases:**
- URLs with timestamp params (?t=) ‚Äî ignored, only v param extracted
- youtu.be with query params (split on ?&)
- Invalid video ID length ‚Äî returns null
- Malformed URLs caught in try-catch, falls back to regex

---

#### 4. **competition-workout-card.tsx** (Client Component)
**What it does:**
- Card-based UI showing workout event details (scheme, time cap, venue, description)
- Displays submission status badge (Submitted, Submit Score, Submission Closed, etc.)
- Shows division-specific scaling when user is registered for that division
- Responsive design with movements/tags sidebar
- CTA button shows appropriate action based on registration + submission status

**Key exports:**
- `CompetitionWorkoutCard` component
- `SubmissionStatus` interface

**Business logic to test:**
- `getCtaConfig()`: determines button label/icon/variant based on registration + submission status
- Time cap formatting (seconds ‚Üí M:SS)
- Scheme labels: "For Time (Capped)" if timeCap present, "For Time" otherwise
- Points multiplier display only if !== 100
- Rounds display only if > 1
- Division-specific description shown only when explicitly selected (no fallback)
- Badge styling: green for submitted (open or closed window), disabled for submission closed

**Edge cases:**
- Not registered ‚Üí "View Details" button regardless of submission status
- Registered but no submission tracking (in-person) ‚Üí "View Details" 
- Submitted + window closed ‚Üí "View Submission" (read-only, no edit)
- Submitted + window open ‚Üí "Edit Submission"
- Not submitted + window open ‚Üí "Submit Score"
- Not submitted + window closed ‚Üí "Submission Closed" (disabled)

---

#### 5. **video-submission-fns.ts** (Server Functions)
**What it does:**
- `getVideoSubmissionFn`: fetches submission data, workout details, existing scores, window status
- `submitVideoFn`: creates/updates video submission + saves claimed score
- `getBatchSubmissionStatusFn`: batch-checks submission status for multiple workouts
- Server-side validation, window checking, registration validation
- Score encoding/parsing with proper handling of cap status and secondary values

**Key exports:**
- `getVideoSubmissionFn` (GET)
- `submitVideoFn` (POST)
- `getBatchSubmissionStatusFn` (GET)

**Business logic to test:**
- **Authentication**: all functions require valid session; return specific errors if not authenticated
- **Registration check**: user must have non-removed registration for competition
- **Competition type check**: submissions only allowed for "online" competitions
- **Submission window check**:
  - No event record = allow submission (backward compatibility)
  - No window configured = allow submission
  - Now < opensAt = "has not opened yet"
  - Now > closesAt = "has closed"
  - Now between = allowed
- **Score encoding**: uses `parseScore()` then `encodeScore()` to validate format
- **Status derivation (server-side, ignores client)**:
  - time-with-cap + encoded >= timeCap * 1000 = "cap"
  - Over-cap submissions normalized to exactly cap time
  - Secondary score only parsed when status="cap"
- **Tiebreak**: validated via `encodeScore()` with tiebreak scheme
- **Sort key computation**: includes secondary_value and tiebreak for proper ordering
- **Score upsert**: `onDuplicateKeyUpdate` to handle re-submissions
- **Team ownership**: tracks ownerTeamId from track
- **Schema type casting**: scheme, scoreType, tiebreakScheme as proper enum types

**Edge cases:**
- Invalid score format throws: "Invalid score format: {error}"
- Invalid tiebreak throws: "Invalid tiebreak score format: {score}"
- Workout not found throws: "Workout not found"
- Team ownership not found throws: "Could not determine team ownership"
- Secondary score: non-negative integer only; invalid values silently ignored
- Tiebreak: encodeScore returns null on invalid format ‚Üí throws error
- Video submission on update: replaces videoUrl, notes, updatedAt timestamp

---

#### 6. **url.ts** (Utility)
**What it does:**
- Simple URL safety check

**Key exports:**
- `isSafeUrl()` function

**Business logic to test:**
- Returns `true` only if URL protocol is "http:" or "https:"
- Returns `false` if URL parsing fails (invalid syntax)
- Returns `false` for other protocols (javascript:, data:, etc.)

**Edge cases:**
- Malformed URLs throw in try-catch ‚Üí returns false
- No protocol (e.g., "example.com") ‚Üí URL constructor throws ‚Üí returns false

---

#### 7. **constants.ts** (Configuration)
**Video-related parts:**
- No video-specific constants; general SITE_DOMAIN/SITE_URL, AUTH, WORKOUT_SCHEMES, SCORE_TYPES, TIEBREAK_SCHEMES

---

### Test Structure & Patterns

**Test directory structure:**
```
test/
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ server-fns/
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ video-submission-fns.test.ts (82 tests)
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ video-validation-fns.test.ts

```

**Test patterns to follow:**

1. **Setup & Mocks** (`test/setup.ts`):
   - Polyfill Web Crypto for node environment
   - Mock DOM element methods (scrollIntoView, pointerCapture)
   - Create chainable mock for Drizzle queries

2. **Server function tests** (`video-submission-fns.test.ts`):
   - Use `FakeDrizzleDb` from @repo/test-utils

   - Use factories: `createTestRegistration()`, `createTestWorkout()`, etc.
   - Mock session via `getSessionFromCookie`
   - Chain `.limit()` mocks in order for each query
   - Test input validation via Zod schema
   - Test auth/authorization checks first

   - Test error messages match expected strings

3. **Schema validation tests** (`video-url.test.ts`):
   - Use `safeParse()` for non-throwing validation
   - Test platform detection: youtube, vimeo, streamable
   - Test URL variations for each platform
   - Test error messages via `VIDEO_URL_ERRORS` enum
   - Test edge cases: whitespace, http vs https, query params

4. **Hook/Component tests** (`use-video-url-validation.test.tsx`):
   - Use `renderHook()` with `waitFor()`
   - Test required vs optional field behavior
   - Test state updates and pending states

---

### Critical Business Logic Points

1. **Score Status Derivation** (server-side only):
   - Ignores client-provided scoreStatus
   - For time-with-cap: any encoded value >= cap becomes "cap" status
   - Over-cap times normalized to exactly cap (no buffer)

2. **Secondary Score** (reps at cap):
   - Only validated/saved when status="cap"
   - Must be non-negative integer
   - Invalid values silently ignored (not throwing)

3. **Tiebreak**:
   - Only saved when tiebreakScheme exists

   - Stored as milliseconds (if time scheme) or raw number

4. **Video URL Safety**:

6. **Registration & Competition Type**:

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/zacjones/Documents/02.Areas/wodsmith/thewodapp-2/.claude/skills/test

# Testing (Router Skill)

**"Write tests. Not too many. Mostly integration."** ‚Äî Kent C. Dodds

This skill routes you to the right testing approach. TDD is non-negotiable for swarm work.

## Testing Trophy

```
      /\
     /  \  E2E (slow, high confidence)
    /----\  5-10 critical path tests
   / INT  \ Integration (SWEET SPOT)
  /--------\ Test real interactions
 |  UNIT  | Unit (fast, focused)
 |________| Pure logic, no mocks
  STATIC   Lint + TypeScript
```

**Philosophy:**
- **Static** catches typos and type errors (free, instant feedback)
- **Unit** tests pure logic in isolation (fast, focused, many tests)
- **Integration** tests real interactions (sweet spot, highest ROI)
- **E2E** tests critical user journeys (slow, expensive, few tests)

## Decision Tree: Which Test Type?

```
What are you testing?
‚îÇ
‚îú‚îÄ Pure function / calculation / business logic?
‚îÇ  ‚îî‚îÄ Use: unit-test (fast, no mocking needed)
‚îÇ     When: calculateScore(), formatDate(), pure validators
‚îÇ
‚îú‚îÄ Multi-component workflow / database operations / React with providers?
‚îÇ  ‚îî‚îÄ Use: integration-test (real interactions, less mocking)
‚îÇ     When: server actions, form submissions, API endpoints, context-heavy components
‚îÇ
‚îî‚îÄ Critical user journey / revenue flow / authentication?
   ‚îî‚îÄ Use: e2e-test (full stack, highest confidence)
      When: signup, checkout, core product actions (only happy paths!)
```

**Heuristic:** If you need >3 mocks, use integration. If it's a critical revenue path, use E2E.

## TDD Red-Green-Refactor (Non-Negotiable)

```
‚îå‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îê
‚îÇ RED: Write failing test  ‚îÇ ‚Üê If it passes, test is wrong
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚î¨‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îò
           ‚îÇ
‚îå‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚ñº‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îê
‚îÇ GREEN: Make it pass      ‚îÇ ‚Üê Minimum code, hardcode OK
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚î¨‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îò
           ‚îÇ
‚îå‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚ñº‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îê
‚îÇ REFACTOR: Clean up       ‚îÇ ‚Üê Tests stay green
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚î¨‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îò
           ‚îÇ
           ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îê
                     ‚îÇ
                     ‚ñº REPEAT
```

**Every feature. Every bug fix. No exceptions.**

## Quick Start by Type

### Unit Test (Pure Logic)

```bash
skills_use(name="unit-test")
```

**Use when:** Testing service functions, pure calculations, webhook handlers
**Key patterns:** Arrange-Act-Assert, one assertion per concept, mock at boundaries only
**Speed:** Fastest (<1ms per test)

### Integration Test (Real Interactions)

```bash
skills_use(name="integration-test")
```

**Use when:** Testing workflows, database ops, React components with providers, server actions
**Key patterns:** Factory data, MSW for network, custom render with providers, `waitFor` for async
**Speed:** Fast (10-100ms per test)

### E2E Test (Critical Paths)

```bash
skills_use(name="e2e-test")
```

**Use when:** Testing signup, revenue flows, multi-step journeys, critical user paths
**Key patterns:** Page objects, fixtures for auth, visual regression, test isolation
**Speed:** Slow (1-10s per test)

## Anti-Patterns to Avoid

‚ùå **Testing implementation details** - Test behavior, not private methods or internal state
‚ùå **Mocking everything** - Prefer real implementations, mock only boundaries (DB, network)
‚ùå **One assertion per test dogma** - Multiple assertions OK if testing same concept
‚ùå **No tests on bug fixes** - Write failing test that reproduces bug, then fix (prevents regression)
‚ùå **Skipping TDD for "simple" changes** - Simple changes break things. Write test first.
‚ùå **Too many E2E tests** - E2E is expensive. Use integration tests for most cases.
‚ùå **Tests that depend on each other** - Each test must be independent, run in any order

## When to Use Which Child Skill

| Scenario | Load This Skill |
|----------|----------------|
| Testing pure functions, calculations, formatters | `unit-test` |
| Testing database queries, server actions, API routes | `integration-test` |
| Testing React components with hooks/context | `integration-test` |
| Testing signup, checkout, critical user flows | `e2e-test` |
| Breaking dependencies in legacy code | `testing-patterns` |
| Understanding characterization tests, seams | `testing-patterns` |

## Testing Patterns (Advanced)

For dependency breaking, seams, characterization tests, and safe refactoring:

```bash
skills_use(name="testing-patterns")
```

**Use when:** Adding tests to legacy code, breaking hard dependencies, understanding code through tests

## Core Principles

1. **Test behavior, not implementation** - Public API, not private methods
2. **Mock at boundaries only** - Database, network, file system. Not your own code.
3. **Fail fast, fail clear** - Good test names describe the expected behavior
4. **Tests are documentation** - Test names should read like specifications
5. **Refactor with confidence** - Tests enable safe changes

## Resources

- Child skills: `unit-test`, `integration-test`, `e2e-test`, `testing-patterns`
- Testing Trophy: https://kentcdodds.com/blog/write-tests
- TDD by Example: Kent Beck
- Working Effectively with Legacy Code: Michael Feathers (for `testing-patterns`)

new Date(date) : date return new Intl.DateTimeFormat("en-US", { weekday: "short", month: "short", day: "numeric", hour: "numeric", minute: "2-digit", timeZone: timezone ?? undefined, }).format(d) } function formatTiebreakTime(milliseconds: number): string { const totalSeconds = Math.floor(milliseconds / 1000) const minutes = Math.floor(totalSeconds / 60) const seconds = totalSeconds % 60 return `${minutes}:${seconds.toString().padStart(2, "0")}` } function getSchemeLabel(scheme: WorkoutScheme): string { switch (scheme) { case "time": case "time-with-cap": return "Time" case "rounds-reps": return "Rounds + Reps" case "reps": return "Reps" case "load": return "Load" case "calories": return "Calories" case "meters": return "Meters" case "feet": return "Feet" case "points": return "Points" case "emom": return "Time" case "pass-fail": return "Rounds Passed" default: return "Score" } } export function VideoSubmissionPreview({ submission, score, workout, canEdit, editReason, timezone, onEdit, }: VideoSubmissionPreviewProps) { const isYouTube = isYouTubeUrl(submission.videoUrl) const hasUpdated = new Date(submission.updatedAt).getTime() !== new Date(submission.submittedAt).getTime() return (

content.thinking:
Good. There are already tests for video submission server functions, video URL schema validation, and the video URL validation hook. But there are no tests for:

1‚Üíimport {webcrypto} from 'node:crypto'
     2‚Üíimport {expect, vi} from 'vitest'
     3‚Üíimport * as matchers from '@testing-library/jest-dom/matchers'
     4‚Üí
     5‚Üí// Extend vitest's expect with jest-dom matchers
     6‚Üíexpect.extend(matchers)
     7‚Üí
     8‚Üí// Polyfill Web Crypto API for Node.js/jsdom environment
     9‚Üí// This is needed for password hashing tests that use crypto.subtle
    10‚Üí// jsdom provides crypto.getRandomValues but not crypto.subtle
    11‚Üíif (!globalThis.crypto?.subtle) {
    12‚Üí  Object.defineProperty(globalThis, 'crypto', {
    13‚Üí    value: webcrypto,
    14‚Üí    writable: true,
    15‚Üí    configurable: true,
    16‚Üí  })
    17‚Üí}
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí// Mock DOM methods used by Radix UI components
    20‚ÜíElement.prototype.scrollIntoView = vi.fn()
    21‚ÜíElement.prototype.hasPointerCapture = vi.fn()
    22‚ÜíElement.prototype.setPointerCapture = vi.fn()
    23‚ÜíElement.prototype.releasePointerCapture = vi.fn()
    24‚Üí
    25‚Üí// Mock the D1 client used by Drizzle
    26‚Üíconst mockD1Client = {
    27‚Üí  prepare: () => mockD1Client,
    28‚Üí  bind: () => mockD1Client,
    29‚Üí  run: () => Promise.resolve({success: true}),
    30‚Üí  all: () =>
    31‚Üí    Promise.resolve({
    32‚Üí      success: true,
    33‚Üí      results: [{id: 'test_team_id', name: 'Test Team', slug: 'test-team'}],
    34‚Üí    }),
    35‚Üí  get: () => Promise.resolve({success: true}),
    36‚Üí  raw: () => Promise.resolve([]),
    37‚Üí  returning: () =>
    38‚Üí    Promise.resolve([
    39‚Üí      {id: 'test_team_id', name: 'Test Team', slug: 'test-team'},
    40‚Üí    ]),
    41‚Üí}
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üí// Create a table mock with findFirst and findMany
    44‚Üíconst createTableMock = () => ({
    45‚Üí  findFirst: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue(null),
    46‚Üí  findMany: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue([]),
    47‚Üí})
    48‚Üí
    49‚Üí// Create query mock with all tables used by db.query.tableName pattern
    50‚Üíconst createQueryMock = () => ({
    51‚Üí  // Core tables
    52‚Üí  workouts: createTableMock(),
    53‚Üí  teamTable: createTableMock(),
    54‚Üí  teamMembershipTable: createTableMock(),
    55‚Üí  userTable: createTableMock(),
    56‚Üí  planTable: createTableMock(),
    57‚Üí  featureTable: createTableMock(),
    58‚Üí  limitTable: createTableMock(),
    59‚Üí  // Competition tables
    60‚Üí  competitionsTable: createTableMock(),
    61‚Üí  competitionRegistrationsTable: createTableMock(),
    62‚Üí  competitionRegistrationQuestionsTable: createTableMock(),
    63‚Üí  competitionJudgeRotationsTable: createTableMock(),
    64‚Üí  competitionHeatsTable: createTableMock(),
    65‚Üí  // Organizer tables
    66‚Üí  organizerRequestTable: createTableMock(),
    67‚Üí  // Sponsor tables
    68‚Üí  sponsorsTable: createTableMock(),
    69‚Üí  sponsorGroupsTable: createTableMock(),
    70‚Üí  // Waiver tables
    71‚Üí  waiversTable: createTableMock(),
    72‚Üí  waiverSignaturesTable: createTableMock(),
    73‚Üí  // Judging tables
    74‚Üí  eventJudgingSheetsTable: createTableMock(),

    76‚Üí  // Video/submission tables
    77‚Üí  videoSubmissionsTable: createTableMock(),
    78‚Üí  // Resource tables
    79‚Üí  eventResourcesTable: createTableMock(),
    80‚Üí  // Address tables
    81‚Üí  addressesTable: createTableMock(),
    82‚Üí  // Commerce tables
    83‚Üí  commerceProductTable: createTableMock(),
    84‚Üí  commercePurchaseTable: createTableMock(),
    85‚Üí  // Entitlement tables
    86‚Üí  entitlementTable: createTableMock(),
    87‚Üí  teamFeatureEntitlementTable: createTableMock(),
    88‚Üí  teamLimitEntitlementTable: createTableMock(),
    89‚Üí  teamEntitlementOverrideTable: createTableMock(),
    90‚Üí  // Scheduling tables
    91‚Üí  scheduledWorkoutInstancesTable: createTableMock(),
    92‚Üí  // Notification tables
    93‚Üí  notificationReservationsTable: createTableMock(),
    94‚Üí})
    95‚Üí
    96‚Üí// Mock the db object that is null in test environment
    97‚Üí// The mockDb needs to be thenable so that when you await the query chain, it returns an array
    98‚Üíexport const createChainableMock = () => {
    99‚Üí  const mock: Record<string, unknown> = {
   100‚Üí    // Make it thenable so await works at any point in the chain
   101‚Üí    then: (resolve: (value: unknown[]) => void) => {
   102‚Üí      resolve([])
   103‚Üí      return Promise.resolve([])
   104‚Üí    },
   105‚Üí    // Query chain methods
   106‚Üí    select: vi.fn(() => createChainableMock()),
   107‚Üí    from: vi.fn(() => createChainableMock()),
   108‚Üí    leftJoin: vi.fn(() => createChainableMock()),
   109‚Üí    innerJoin: vi.fn(() => createChainableMock()),
   110‚Üí    where: vi.fn(() => createChainableMock()),
   111‚Üí    limit: vi.fn(() => createChainableMock()),
   112‚Üí    orderBy: vi.fn(() => createChainableMock()),
   113‚Üí    offset: vi.fn(() => createChainableMock()),
   114‚Üí    groupBy: vi.fn(() => createChainableMock()),
   115‚Üí    // Insert/update chain
   116‚Üí    insert: vi.fn(() => createChainableMock()),
   117‚Üí    values: vi.fn(() => createChainableMock()),
   118‚Üí    returning: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue([{id: 'test_id', name: 'Test'}]),
   119‚Üí    onDuplicateKeyUpdate: vi.fn(() => createChainableMock()),
   120‚Üí    // Update chain
   121‚Üí    update: vi.fn(() => createChainableMock()),
   122‚Üí    set: vi.fn(() => createChainableMock()),
   123‚Üí    // Delete
   124‚Üí    delete: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue({changes: 0}),
   125‚Üí    // Transaction support
   126‚Üí    transaction: vi.fn(async (fn: (tx: Record<string, unknown>) => Promise<unknown>) => {
   127‚Üí      const txMock = createChainableMock()
   128‚Üí      return fn(txMock)
   129‚Üí    }),
   130‚Üí    // Other methods
   131‚Üí    get: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue(null),
   132‚Üí    // Query API (drizzle relational queries)
   133‚Üí    query: createQueryMock(),
   134‚Üí  }
   135‚Üí  return mock
   136‚Üí}
   137‚Üí
   138‚Üíconst mockDb = createChainableMock()
   139‚Üí
   140‚Üívi.mock('@/db', () => ({
   141‚Üí  db: null,
   142‚Üí  getDb: vi.fn(() => mockDb),
   143‚Üí}))
   144‚Üí

content.thinking:
Now I have a comprehensive understanding of:

1. The branch's changes
2. Existing tests (server function tests are well-covered)
3. Testing patterns used in the codebase

The gaps I should fill with tests:

1. **`url.ts` - `isSafeUrl()`** - Pure unit test, no existing tests
2. **`youtube-embed.tsx` - `extractYouTubeVideoId()` and `isYouTubeUrl()`** - Pure unit tests, no existing tests
3. **`competition-workout-card.tsx` - `getCtaConfig()`, `formatTime()`, `getSchemeLabel()`** - Pure unit tests, no existing tests


4. **`video-submission-preview.tsx` - `formatTiebreakTime()`, `getSchemeLabel()`** - Pure unit tests, no existing tests

I'm realizing most of these helper functions are internal to their components, so I'll need to test the exported functions where possible‚Äîlike `isSafeUrl()` and `isYouTubeUrl()`‚Äîwhile considering whether testing internal functions through component integration tests makes more sense. indirectly)
- For `getCtaConfig()` - not exported, but I could test it through the component rendering or I could export it for testing
- For component rendering - I could use component tests with Testing Library

Actually, looking more carefully at the codebase patterns, the `extractYouTubeVideoId` is not exported but `isYouTubeUrl` is. I can test `isYouTubeUrl` as a proxy for the extraction logic. But I should also test the extraction function directly since it's the core logic.

For the component functions like `getCtaConfig`, `formatTime`, `getSchemeLabel` - these are internal functions. The best approach per the testing philosophy is to test through the component's public API (rendering). But given that these are pure functions with complex business logic, it might be better to extract and export them for direct testing.

Looking at the actual files, I see there are several internal utility functions scattered across `competition-workout-card.tsx` and `video-submission-preview.tsx` - things like `formatTime`, `getSchemeLabel`, and `getCtaConfig` that handle complex formatting and configuration logic. The testing philosophy suggests testing through rendering, but for these pure functions with intricate business logic, direct unit tests make more sense. I'll take a pragmatic approach: test the exported utilities like `isSafeUrl` and `isYouTubeUrl` directly, and handle the YouTube extraction indirectly through component rendering or by testing the wrapper function. Now I'm mapping out the test structure across three component files‚ÄîYouTube embed parsing, competition workout card logic, and video submission preview rendering‚Äîand I'll write them all together.

   178‚Üí/**
   179‚Üí * Normalize US state name to abbreviation
   180‚Üí * @param state - State name or abbreviation
   181‚Üí * @returns State abbreviation or original value if not found
   182‚Üí */
   183‚Üíexport function normalizeState(
   184‚Üí	state: string | null | undefined,
   185‚Üí): string | null {
   186‚Üí	if (!state) return null
   187‚Üí
   188‚Üí	const trimmed = state.trim()
   189‚Üí	const normalized = trimmed.toLowerCase()
   190‚Üí
   191‚Üí	// If already an abbreviation (2 uppercase letters), return as-is
   192‚Üí	if (/^[A-Z]{2}$/.test(trimmed)) {
   193‚Üí		return trimmed
   194‚Üí	}
   195‚Üí
   196‚Üí	// Look up in mapping
   197‚Üí	const abbr = US_STATE_ABBREVIATIONS[normalized]
   198‚Üí	return abbr || trimmed
   199‚Üí}
   200‚Üí
   201‚Üí/**
   202‚Üí * Normalize country name to ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code
   203‚Üí * @param country - Country name or code
   204‚Üí * @returns Country code or original value if not found
   205‚Üí */
   206‚Üíexport function normalizeCountry(
   207‚Üí	country: string | null | undefined,
   208‚Üí): string | null {
   209‚Üí	if (!country) return null
   210‚Üí
   211‚Üí	const trimmed = country.trim()
   212‚Üí	const normalized = trimmed.toLowerCase()
   213‚Üí
   214‚Üí	// Look up in mapping first (handles "UK" -> "GB")
   215‚Üí	const code = COUNTRY_CODE_MAP[normalized]
   216‚Üí	if (code) return code
   217‚Üí
   218‚Üí	// If already a 2-letter code, return uppercase
   219‚Üí	if (/^[a-z]{2}$/i.test(trimmed)) {
   220‚Üí		return trimmed.toUpperCase()
   221‚Üí	}
   222‚Üí
   223‚Üí	// Return original if not found
   224‚Üí	return trimmed
   225‚Üí}
   226‚Üí
   227‚Üí/**
   228‚Üí * Get display name for country code
   229‚Üí * @param countryCode - ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code
   230‚Üí * @returns Display name or country code if not found
   231‚Üí */
   232‚Üíexport function getCountryDisplayName(
   233‚Üí	countryCode: string | null | undefined,
   234‚Üí): string | null {
   235‚Üí	if (!countryCode) return null
   236‚Üí
   237‚Üí	const normalized = countryCode.trim().toUpperCase()
   238‚Üí	return COUNTRY_DISPLAY_NAMES[normalized] || countryCode
   239‚Üí}
   240‚Üí
   241‚Üí/**
   242‚Üí * Check if address has any meaningful data
   243‚Üí * @param address - Address object (can be partial or null)
   244‚Üí * @returns true if address has at least one field with data
   245‚Üí */
   246‚Üíexport function hasAddressData(
   247‚Üí	address: Partial<Address> | null | undefined,
   248‚Üí): address is Partial<Address> {
   249‚Üí	if (!address) return false
   250‚Üí
   251‚Üí	return Boolean(
   252‚Üí		address.name?.trim() ||
   253‚Üí			address.streetLine1?.trim() ||
   254‚Üí			address.streetLine2?.trim() ||
   255‚Üí			address.city?.trim() ||
   256‚Üí			address.stateProvince?.trim() ||
   257‚Üí			address.postalCode?.trim() ||
   258‚Üí			address.countryCode?.trim(),
   259‚Üí	)
   260‚Üí}
   261‚Üí
   262‚Üí/**
   263‚Üí * Format city and state/country line
   264‚Üí * - US addresses: "Austin, TX" (city + state)
   265‚Üí * - Non-US addresses: "London, UK" (city + country)
   266‚Üí * @param address - Address object (can be partial or null)
   267‚Üí * @returns Formatted city line or null if insufficient data
   268‚Üí */
   269‚Üíexport function formatCityLine(
   270‚Üí	address: Partial<Address> | null | undefined,
   271‚Üí): string | null {
   272‚Üí	if (!address?.city?.trim()) return null
   273‚Üí
   274‚Üí	const city = address.city.trim()
   275‚Üí	const state = address.stateProvince?.trim()
   276‚Üí	const country = address.countryCode?.trim()?.toUpperCase()
   277‚Üí
   278‚Üí	// For US addresses, show city + state
   279‚Üí	if (country === "US" || (!country && state)) {
   280‚Üí		if (state) {
   281‚Üí			return `${city}, ${state}`
   282‚Üí		}
   283‚Üí		return city
   284‚Üí	}
   285‚Üí
   286‚Üí	// For non-US addresses, show city + country
   287‚Üí	if (country) {
   288‚Üí		return `${city}, ${country}`
   289‚Üí	}
   290‚Üí
   291‚Üí	// Fallback: city + state if available, otherwise just city
   292‚Üí	if (state) {
   293‚Üí		return `${city}, ${state}`
   294‚Üí	}
   295‚Üí
   296‚Üí	return city
   297‚Üí}
   298‚Üí
   299‚Üí/**
   300‚Üí * Format full address with all available components
   301‚Üí * @param address - Address object (can be partial or null)
   302‚Üí * @returns Formatted full address or null if no data
   303‚Üí */
   304‚Üíexport function formatFullAddress(
   305‚Üí	address: Partial<Address> | null | undefined,
   306‚Üí): string | null {
   307‚Üí	if (!hasAddressData(address)) return null
   308‚Üí
   309‚Üí	const parts: string[] = []
   310‚Üí
   311‚Üí	// Name (venue/location name)
   312‚Üí	if (address.name?.trim()) {
   313‚Üí		parts.push(address.name.trim())
   314‚Üí	}
   315‚Üí
   316‚Üí	// Street lines
   317‚Üí	if (address.streetLine1?.trim()) {
   318‚Üí		parts.push(address.streetLine1.trim())
   319‚Üí	}
   320‚Üí	if (address.streetLine2?.trim()) {
   321‚Üí		parts.push(address.streetLine2.trim())
   322‚Üí	}
   323‚Üí
   324‚Üí	// City, State/Province Postal
   325‚Üí	const cityParts: string[] = []
   326‚Üí	if (address.city?.trim()) {
   327‚Üí		cityParts.push(address.city.trim())
   328‚Üí	}
   329‚Üí	if (address.stateProvince?.trim()) {
   330‚Üí		cityParts.push(address.stateProvince.trim())
   331‚Üí	}
   332‚Üí
   333‚Üí	// Add postal code to the last part (state or city)
   334‚Üí	if (address.postalCode?.trim() && cityParts.length > 0) {
   335‚Üí		const lastIndex = cityParts.length - 1
   336‚Üí		cityParts[lastIndex] =
   337‚Üí			`${cityParts[lastIndex]} ${address.postalCode.trim()}`
   338‚Üí	} else if (address.postalCode?.trim()) {
   339‚Üí		// No city or state, just postal code
   340‚Üí		cityParts.push(address.postalCode.trim())
   341‚Üí	}
   342‚Üí
   343‚Üí	if (cityParts.length > 0) {
   344‚Üí		parts.push(cityParts.join(", "))
   345‚Üí	}
   346‚Üí
   347‚Üí	// Country
   348‚Üí	if (address.countryCode?.trim()) {
   349‚Üí		const countryDisplay = getCountryDisplayName(address.countryCode.trim())
   350‚Üí		if (countryDisplay) {
   351‚Üí			parts.push(countryDisplay)
   352‚Üí		}
   353‚Üí	}
   354‚Üí
   355‚Üí	return parts.length > 0 ? parts.join("\n") : null
   356‚Üí}
   357‚Üí
   358‚Üí/**
   359‚Üí * Format location badge display for competition listings
   360‚Üí * Priority: online > city+state > city+country > city > name > team name > "Location TBA"
   361‚Üí * @param address - Address object (can be partial or null)
   362‚Üí * @param competitionType - Competition type ('in-person' or 'online')
   363‚Üí * @param organizingTeamName - Fallback team name if no address data
   364‚Üí * @returns LocationBadgeDisplay with text and icon
   365‚Üí */
   366‚Üíexport function formatLocationBadge(
   367‚Üí	address: Partial<Address> | null | undefined,
   368‚Üí	competitionType: "in-person" | "online",
   369‚Üí	organizingTeamName?: string | null,
   370‚Üí): LocationBadgeDisplay {
   371‚Üí	// Online competitions always show globe icon with "Online"
   372‚Üí	if (competitionType === "online") {
   373‚Üí		return {
   374‚Üí			text: "Online",
   375‚Üí			icon: "globe",
   376‚Üí		}
   377‚Üí	}
   378‚Üí
   379‚Üí	// Try to format city line (city + state/country)
   380‚Üí	const cityLine = formatCityLine(address)
   381‚Üí	if (cityLine) {
   382‚Üí		return {
   383‚Üí			text: cityLine,
   384‚Üí			icon: "map-pin",
   385‚Üí		}
   386‚Üí	}
   387‚Üí
   388‚Üí	// Just city
   389‚Üí	if (address?.city?.trim()) {
   390‚Üí		return {
   391‚Üí			text: address.city.trim(),
   392‚Üí			icon: "map-pin",
   393‚Üí		}
   394‚Üí	}
   395‚Üí
   396‚Üí	// Venue/location name
   397‚Üí	if (address?.name?.trim()) {
   398‚Üí		return {
   399‚Üí			text: address.name.trim(),
   400‚Üí			icon: "map-pin",
   401‚Üí		}
   402‚Üí	}
   403‚Üí
   404‚Üí	// Fallback to organizing team name
   405‚Üí	if (organizingTeamName?.trim()) {
   406‚Üí		return {
   407‚Üí			text: organizingTeamName.trim(),
   408‚Üí			icon: "map-pin",
   409‚Üí		}
   410‚Üí	}
   411‚Üí
   412‚Üí	// No location data
   413‚Üí	return {
   414‚Üí		text: "Location TBA",
   415‚Üí		icon: "map-pin",
   416‚Üí	}
   417‚Üí}
   418‚Üí
   419‚Üí/**
   420‚Üí * Normalize address input by standardizing state and country codes
   421‚Üí * @param input - Raw address input
   422‚Üí * @returns Normalized address input
   423‚Üí */
   424‚Üíexport function normalizeAddressInput(
   425‚Üí	input: Partial<Address>,
   426‚Üí): Partial<Address> {
   427‚Üí	return {
   428‚Üí		...input,
   429‚Üí		stateProvince: normalizeState(input.stateProvince),
   430‚Üí		countryCode: normalizeCountry(input.countryCode),
   431‚Üí	}
   432‚Üí}
   433‚Üí
   434‚Üí/**
   435‚Üí * Generate a Google Maps navigation URL from an address
   436‚Üí * @param address - Address object (can be partial or null)
   437‚Üí * @returns Google Maps search URL or null if no meaningful address data
   438‚Üí */
   439‚Üíexport function getGoogleMapsUrl(
   440‚Üí	address: Partial<Address> | null | undefined,
   441‚Üí): string | null {
   442‚Üí	if (!hasAddressData(address)) return null
   443‚Üí
   444‚Üí	const parts: string[] = []
   445‚Üí
   446‚Üí	// Build address string from available components
   447‚Üí	if (address.streetLine1?.trim()) {
   448‚Üí		parts.push(address.streetLine1.trim())
   449‚Üí	}
   450‚Üí	if (address.city?.trim()) {
   451‚Üí		parts.push(address.city.trim())
   452‚Üí	}
   453‚Üí	if (address.stateProvince?.trim()) {
   454‚Üí		parts.push(address.stateProvince.trim())
   455‚Üí	}
   456‚Üí	if (address.postalCode?.trim()) {
   457‚Üí		parts.push(address.postalCode.trim())
   458‚Üí	}
   459‚Üí	if (address.countryCode?.trim()) {
   460‚Üí		const countryDisplay = getCountryDisplayName(address.countryCode.trim())
   461‚Üí		if (countryDisplay) {
   462‚Üí			parts.push(countryDisplay)
   463‚Üí		}
   464‚Üí	}
   465‚Üí
   466‚Üí	if (parts.length === 0) return null
   467‚Üí
   468‚Üí	const addressString = parts.join(", ")
   469‚Üí	const encodedAddress = encodeURIComponent(addressString)
   470‚Üí	return `https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=${encodedAddress}`
   471‚Üí}

content.text:
All 72 new tests pass across 4 test files. Full suite: **2249 tests, 77 files, all green.**

### New test files created:

**`test/utils/url.test.ts`** (7 tests)
- `isSafeUrl` ‚Äî http/https pass, javascript:/data:/ftp:/file: rejected, malformed URLs rejected

**`test/components/youtube-embed.test.tsx`** (19 tests)
- `isYouTubeUrl` ‚Äî watch?v=, youtu.be, /embed/, /shorts/, query params, non-YouTube rejection, empty/invalid
- `YouTubeEmbed` ‚Äî iframe rendering, custom titles, sandbox attrs, external link fallback, unsafe URL protection
- `YouTubeThumbnail` ‚Äî thumbnail image rendering, placeholder for non-YouTube

**`test/components/competition-workout-card.test.tsx`** (23 tests)
- CTA states ‚Äî all 5 submission status combinations (not registered, in-person, submit/edit/view/closed)
- Scheme labels ‚Äî time, time-with-cap, amrap, load, emom
- Time cap formatting ‚Äî M:SS display, null handling
- Points multiplier ‚Äî shown when ‚âÝ100, hidden when 100 or null
- Rounds display ‚Äî shown when >1, hidden when 1
- Track order padding, descriptions, division scaling

**`test/components/video-submission-preview.test.tsx`** (23 tests)
- Video display ‚Äî YouTube embed vs external link, unsafe URL protection
- Score display ‚Äî scheme labels for all workout types, capped badge, secondary value, tiebreak formatting
- Notes ‚Äî shown/hidden
- Edit button ‚Äî canEdit + onEdit gating
- Timestamps ‚Äî submitted vs updated, duplicate suppression
- Edit status banners ‚Äî open window, closed with reason, default closed message
